questions.question: Anushilan Samiti was established in-
questions.explanation: Some of the revolutionary organizations in India: • Vyayam Mandala: 1896-97 (Poona) • Mitra Mela: 1901(Nasik) • Abhinava Bharata: 1904 (Poona) • Swadesh Bandhav Samiti: 1905(Warisal) • Bharat Mata Society: 1907 (Punjab) • Hindustan Republican Society: 1924 (Kanpur) • Bharat Naujawan Sabha: 1926 (Lahore) • Hindustan Socialist Republican Association: 1928 (Delhi)

questions.question: Who among the following was involved in the attempt to murder case of Judge Kingsford in 1908?
questions.explanation: Some of the revolutionary cases / events during the course of Indian national movement: • Murder of Rand and Amherst: Chapekar brothers, Damodar and Balakrishna (1897, Poona) • Maniktalla and Alipor Bomb conspiracy case: Aurobindo Ghosh (1908, Calcutta) • Murder of Jackson: Anant Karkare (1909, Nasik) • Murder of Curzon Wyllie: Madan Lal Dhingra (1909, London) • Attempt to murder Hardinge: Ras Behari Bose and Basant Kumar (1912, Delhi) • Kakori train dacoity case: Ram Prasad Bismil and Ashafaqulla (1925, Kakori, UP) • Murder of Saunders: Bhagat Singh (1928, Lahore) • Assembly bomb case: Bhagat Singh and Batukeshwar Dutta (1929, Delhi)

questions.question: Mahatma Gandhi was elected the President of Indian National Congress only once in his lifetime in the year 1924. The session was held in-
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi has been an instrumental figure in the Indian freedom struggle and he has been an important figure for the Indian National Congress as well. However, the person was elected the President of the INC only for once in his lifetime and it was during the Belgaum session of INC in Karnataka in the year 1924. Actually, after Indian achieved independence, Gandhi suggested dissolution of INC and conversion of it into Lok Sevak Samaj

questions.question: Who among the following gave the name 'Rashtrapita' to Mahatma Gandhi?
questions.explanation: Some of the names given to Mahatma Gandhi by others are: • Mahatma: RN Tagore in 1917 • Malang Babal Nanga Faqir: Kabailis of North-West Frontier in 1930 • Half-naked saint: Winston Churchill in 1931 • Rashtrapita: Shubhash Chandra Bose in 1944

questions.question: Chauri-Chaura incident is considered an infamous incident in the history of Indian national movement. It is related to which among the following?
questions.explanation: INC decided to go for Non-Cooperation Movement in its Calcutta Session in the year 1920 and defined Swaraj as its ultimate aim. However, an attack on a local police station by angry peasants at Chauri-Chaura, in Gorakhpur disctrict in UP on Feb 5, 1922 changed the situation for the movement. Gandhi called the movement off after this incident, on Feb 12, 1922

questions.question: Who was the British Viceroy during the Second Round Table Conference in 1931?
questions.explanation: The Second Round Table Conference was held in London during the viceroyalty of Lord Willingdon during September to December 1931 and Gandhiji attended the conference on behalf of INC. It failed as Gandhiji did not agree to the policy of communal representation by British PM Ramsay McDonald and as a result, the conference ended on December 1, 1931 without any concrete result out of it

questions.question: Poona Pact was signed in the year-
questions.explanation: The Communal Award announced by British PM Ramsay McDonald in 1932 created immense dissatisfaction among the Hindus. Gandhi, who was on fast in protest staked his life in order to get the Award overturned. According to the pact, the idea of separate electorate for the Depressed Classes was abandoned but seats reserved for them in the provincial legislatures were increased from 71 in the Award to 148 and in the central legislature 18% of the total. The fast ended with Poona Pact which annulled the Award. Gandhiji was representing the Hindus whereas BR Ambedkar was the leader of the Depressed Classes.

questions.question: Jawaharlal Nehru has remarked that "It is a new charter of slavery" regarding which among the following issues?
questions.explanation: Government of India Act 1935 serves as the foundation of Indian Constitution in many ways. The act introduced provincial autonomy, abolished diarchy in provinces and made ministers responsible to the legislative and federation at the centre. The Act was, however, condemned by all the sections of the Indian society

questions.question: Pakistan Resolution was adopted by Muslim League in the Lahore Session held in the year-
questions.explanation: The Lahore session of the All India Muslim League held on 22-24 March 1940 passed the Pakistan resolution. The Resolution rejected the federal system of government as envisaged in the Government of India Act, 1935.

questions.question: Indian National Army had an exclusive brigade only for women. It was named as-
questions.explanation: Indian National Army was mainly known as Azad Hind Fauj in India that remains an integral part of the freedom struggle in India. INA was formed by Shubhash Chandra Bose on October 21, 1943 in Singapore. It had three brigades for fighting and they were named after Subhash, Gandhi and Nehru. Jhansi Rani Brigade was only for women cadets

questions.question: Who was the president of the Constituent Assembly that was formed to formulate a constitution for India?
questions.explanation: After the formation of interim government by Indian National Congress in various provinces of the country in 1946, it went on to form a constituent assembly on December 9, 1946 with the sole objective of making a constitution for free India. They met for the first time on December 9, 1946 and Dr Rajendra Prasad was chosen as the President of the Assembly. The Muslim League, however, did not join the assembly

questions.question: Who was the ruler of India when the 3rd Buddhist Council was held?
questions.explanation: The 3rd Buddhist Council was held in 250 BC in Pataliputra. The Chairman for the council was Mogaliputra Tissa and Ashoka was the patron of the convention. During the convention, the third of the Tripitakas was compiled i.e. the Abhidhamma Pitaka and it was also decided that missionaries would be sent to various parts of the world

questions.question: Who among the following is the author of the book 'Mahavibhasa Shastra'?
questions.explanation: Some of the Sanskrit texts and their authors: • Buddha Charita: Aswaghosa • Sutralankar: Ashwaghosa • Sariputra Prakaran: Aswaghosa • Visudhamagga: Buddhagosha • Madhyamika Karika: Nagarjuna • Prajnaparimita Karika: Nagarjuna

questions.question: Which among the following is the largest Indian site of Indus Valley Civilization?
questions.explanation: The Indus Valley Civilization was spread over Sindh, Baluchistan, Punjab, Haryana, Rajasthan, Gujarat, Western UP and Northern Maharashtra. Most of the prominent sites of the civilization are in Pakistan as of present day. Mohenjodaro is the largest site of the civilization whereas Rakhigarhi is the largest Indian site. Shatughai and Mundigaq are the Indus sites that have been found in Afghanistan

questions.question: Which among the following is considered as the oldest Upanishads?
questions.explanation: The Upanishads are important scriptures of the Rig Vedic ages. They are primarily philosophical texts and are known as Vedanta as they come towards the end of the Veda. There are 108 Upanishads and Vrihadaryanka is the oldest among them.

questions.question: Ayurveda is related to which among the following?
questions.explanation: The Upavedas or the auxiliary Vedas are traditionally related to the Vedas: • Ayurveda: Rigveda • Gandharvaveda: Sama Veda • Dhanurveda: Yajur Veda • Shipaveda / Arthaveda: Atharva Veda

questions.question: Who among the following was the last ruler of the Haryanka Dynasty in Magadha?
questions.explanation: After Udayin, the Haryanka dynasty was not as efficient as it was during the reign of the earlier rulers. Udayin was succeeded by Anuruddha, Munda and Naga-Dasak but all of them were weak as rulers. People got disgusted with Naga-Dasak and elected Shishunag as the King of Magadha

questions.question: Syadvada is related to which among the following?
questions.explanation: Syadvada is a theory propounded in the principles of Jainism and it implies that all our judgments are necessarily relative, conditional and limited. According to Syadvada, seven modes of prediction are possible and absolute affirmation as well as absolute negation both are wrong. It is also known as Anekantvada i.e. the theory of plurality or multi-sidedness

questions.question: The first Jain Council was held in which among the following places?
questions.explanation: 1st Jain Council was held in the year 300 BC in Pataliputra. It was chaired by Sthulabhadra and the patron was Chandragupta Maurya of the Maurya Dynasty. During this convention, compilation of 12 Angas took place that went on to become the sacred text of the Jains

questions.question: 'Indica' was authored by
questions.explanation: Megasthenes was the ambassador of Selukas Nikator in the royal court of Chandragupta Maurya. He wrote the book 'Indica' which served as an important source of information for the Maurya Rule. But its original copy is lost and it is only found in various quotations of various classical writers such as Strabo, Plutarch etc

questions.question: Kalinga War was fought in the year-
questions.explanation: in the 9th year of his coronation, Ashoka fought the Kalinga War in the year 261 BC. The king was moved by the massacre in the war and therefore, abandoned the policy of physical occupation in favour of policy of cultural conquest. In other words, Bherighosa was replaced by Dhammaghosa

questions.question: The rule of the Maurya Dynasty ended in the year-
questions.explanation: After the death of Ashoka, Mauryan empire was divided into two parts-Eastern and Western. The western part was ruled by Kunala and the eastern part was ruled by Dasaratha. The last Mauryan ruler, Brihadratha, was assassinated in the year 185 BC and his commander-in-chief, Pushyamitra Sunga established Sunga dynasty in Magadha

questions.question: Who were the first to issue gold coins in India?
questions.explanation: Indo-Greek rulers were the first foreign rulers of North-Western India in the post-Maurya period. The most famous of them was Menander who ruled from 165 BC to 145 BC and he was also known as Milinda. They were important as they issued large number of coins and also the first ones to issue gold coins. They introduced the Greek features in art giving rise to Gandhar School of art in North-Western India

questions.question: The Saka era was started in the year-
questions.explanation: The most famous Kushan ruler was Kanishka as he was also known as 'Second Ashoka'. He started an era in 78 AD which is now known as Saka era and it is also used by the Government of India for all official purposes now

questions.question: Which of the following is also known as 'Golden Age' of ancient India?
questions.explanation: In the 4th century AD, a new dynasty, the Guptas, arose in Magadha and established a large kingdom over the greater part of Northern India and their rule lasted over 200 years. The age is also known as 'classical Age' or 'Golden Age' of ancient India and was perhaps the most prosperous era in the history of ancient India

questions.question: Samudragupta is also known as Napoleon of India. Who among the following gave him the title?
questions.explanation: Samudragupta was the greatest king in the Gupta dynasty as he ruled for 45 years from 335 AD to 380 AD. He conquered most parts of the country but in the South, he went for only conquering but no annexation of the defeated territories was exercised. His kingdom related details are found in Allahabad Prasasti which was written by his court poet, Harisena. Vincent Smith described him as Napoleon of India for his military success

questions.question: Palas of Eastern India were known as an important component in the Tripartite struggle. Their capital was in which among the following places?
questions.explanation: The Pala Dynasty was established by Gopala in the year 750 AD with their capital being at Muddagiri or Munger. His son, Dharmapala succeeded him and he revived Nalanda University in Bihar and also founded Vikramshila University

questions.question: Dilwara Temple is located in which among the following places?
questions.explanation: During the reign of Rajput rulers, the country remained free from foreign invasions but lost foreign contact as well. The caste system was rigid and they were proud warriors but hospitable people. Many great fortresses were built during their reign and some of the examples are Khajuraho (MP), Lingaraja Temple (Orissa), Sun Temple (Konarak, Orissa), the Jagannath Temple (Puri, Orissa) among others

questions.question: Who among the following Delhi Sultans died while playing Polo?
questions.explanation: Qutubbudin Aibak was the founder of Slave Dynasty and was known as Lakh Baksh as he was very generous and kind hearted. He constructed two mosques- Quwat-ul-Islam at Delhi and Adhai din ka Jhonpra in Ajmer in Rajasthan. He was a great patron of art and learning but died in 1210 while playing Chaugan or Polo. He also started the construction of Qutub Minar

questions.question: Balban introduced the custom of Sijda in his court. It refers to the practice of-
options: Kissing the feet of the monarch
questions.explanation: Balban was instrumental in establishing the supremacy of Sultans in the Delhi Sultanate and he broke the power of Chalisa in order to stabilize the reign of the Sultan above everything else. He introduced the practice of Sijda (prostration before the monarch) and Paibos (kissing the feet of the monarch) as the normal forms of salutation.
